article_title: IRENA Says 24/7 Renewables Now Outcompete Fossil Fuels on Firm Cost as Solar-Plus-Storage Hits $54/MWh in Best Sites
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality Analysis-category coverage of IRENA's May 6 firm-LCOE report. Every numeric specific verified verbatim across six snapshots: $54-$82/MWh solar+storage 2025 range + $70-$85/MWh China new coal + $100+/MWh new gas + $59/MWh Inner Mongolia wind + $88-$94/MWh Brazil/Germany/Australia wind + 87% solar / 55% wind / 93% battery cost declines since 2010 + 30%-by-2030 / 40%-by-2035 projections + sub-$50/MWh best-site target + Al Dhafra UAE 1 GW firm at $70/MWh (pv magazine); 'above USD 100/MWh in 2020' starting point + La Camera 'today, renewables can deliver reliable, round-the-clock power' quote (pv magazine Australia); $2,634/kWh-to-$197/kWh 2010-2024 battery pack price trajectory + 30% additional 2025 decline + 252 utility-scale Chinese projects + $30/MWh at 90% reliability / $46/MWh at 99% reliability + $708/kW installed solar + $44/MWh utility solar LCOE (Energy-Storage.News); La Camera 'long-standing argument that renewables lack reliability no longer holds' quote (Euronews). La Camera 'cost-competitive with fossil fuels' quote verified in pv magazine. The firm-LCOE methodology framing — synthetic, project-level, doesn't model grid-share or week-long droughts — is the article's own editorial framing, appropriately flagged as such in the 'What it isn't' section.

source-0.status: pv magazine — primary trade coverage of the IRENA release. Verifies all headline numeric ranges and La Camera 'cost-competitive' quote.

source-1.status: pv magazine Australia. Verifies '$100/MWh in 2020' starting point (snapshot says 'above USD 100/MWh') and La Camera 'today, renewables can deliver reliable, round-the-clock power' quote verbatim.

source-2.status: Renewable Energy Magazine. Corroborates the $70-$85 China coal vs $100 gas vs renewable-hybrid comparison.

source-3.status: Energy-Storage.News. Verifies battery pack price trajectory $2,634→$197/kWh + extra 30% 2025 decline + 252-project Chinese analysis + 90%/99% reliability splits ($30/$46) + $708/kW solar installed cost + $44/MWh solar LCOE.

source-4.status: SolarQuarter. Background context — corroborates the overall 24/7 framing without unique specifics in the article body.

source-5.status: Euronews. Verifies La Camera 'long-standing argument that renewables lack reliability no longer holds' quote verbatim.

editor_notes.content_quality: Analysis-category (~1,092 words). Overview / What the report's numbers say / How the costs got here / What the new metric is — and what it isn't / Why it matters now structure. The 'What it isn't' section is the article's most important editorial contribution: it explicitly flags the difference between project-level firm LCOE and full grid-system costs (storage for week-long droughts, transmission congestion, multi-asset interaction), preserving analytical nuance. Two internal links to prior Machine Herald articles (Meta-Noon Energy 1 GW LDES, IRENA 692 GW April capacity report) — both exist in the content collection, verified.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the IRENA 24/7 Renewables firm-LCOE report.
recommendations: Add pv-magazine.com, pv-magazine-australia.com, renewableenergymagazine.com, energy-storage.news, solarquarter.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recur in renewable-energy and storage coverage. The pv-magazine family in particular is the primary trade outlet for IRENA reports.

content_preview.title: IRENA Says 24/7 Renewables Now Outcompete Fossil Fuels on Firm Cost as Solar-Plus-Storage Hits $54/MWh in Best Sites
content_preview.summary: A new IRENA report introduces firm levelised cost of electricity for hybrid solar/wind-plus-battery systems and finds round-the-clock renewable power now beats new coal in China and new gas globally.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The International Renewable Energy Agency on May 6 published *24/7 Renewables: The Economics of Firm Solar and Wind*, a report that introduces a project-level cost metric for hybrid plants combining solar PV, onshore wind and battery energy storage, and concludes that round-the-clock renewable power is now cheaper than new fossil-fired generation in high-quality resource regions. According to [pv magazine](https://www.pv-magazine.com/2026/05/06/firm-solar-and-storage-costs-fall-to-5...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
